evals.name: missing-semicolon-diagnosis
evals.prompt: I have a BIRD2 config file at bird-agent/evals/fixtures/bird.conf. When I run `bird -p -c bird.conf` it fails with a syntax error but I don't understand the message. Can you help me find and fix the problem?
evals.expected_output: Run birdcc lint on the file, identify the missing semicolon after 'router id 192.0.2.1', and propose a corrected snippet.

assertions.name: ran_birdcc_lint
assertions.description: The agent invoked birdcc lint on the fixture file.

assertions.name: identified_missing_semicolon
assertions.description: The agent identified the root cause as a missing semicolon after router id 192.0.2.1.

assertions.name: provided_corrected_snippet
assertions.description: The agent provided a corrected config snippet with the semicolon added.

assertions.name: explained_bird_error
assertions.description: The agent explained why bird -p reported the error at line 3 instead of line 1.

assertions.name: did_not_modify_fixture
assertions.description: The agent did not modify the original fixture file.

evals.name: format-preview
evals.prompt: Please format my BIRD config at bird-agent/evals/fixtures/bird-unformatted.conf. I want to see what changes the formatter would make before you actually write them.
evals.expected_output: Run birdcc fmt --check on the file, show the diff or formatted output, and ask for confirmation before running birdcc fmt --write.

assertions.name: ran_fmt_check
assertions.description: The agent invoked birdcc fmt --check on the target file.

assertions.name: showed_diff_or_formatted_output
assertions.description: The agent displayed a diff or the formatted output so the user can preview changes.

assertions.name: indentation_normalized
assertions.description: The preview shows the neighbor line being indented to match the local as line.

assertions.name: did_not_write_without_confirmation
assertions.description: The agent did not modify the original file before receiving explicit confirmation.

evals.name: github-actions-workflow
evals.prompt: How do I add a GitHub Actions workflow to automatically lint and validate BIRD2 configs in my repo? We use pnpm and the main config is at configs/bird2.conf.
evals.expected_output: Provide a working GitHub Actions workflow using setup-birdcc that installs the toolchain, runs birdcc fmt --check, and runs birdcc lint --bird on the specified config path.

assertions.name: uses_setup_birdcc_action
assertions.description: The workflow uses the bird-chinese-community/setup-birdcc GitHub Action.

assertions.name: includes_birdcc_lint
assertions.description: The workflow runs birdcc lint with --bird for runtime validation.

assertions.name: includes_fmt_check
assertions.description: The workflow runs birdcc fmt --check to verify formatting.

assertions.name: targets_correct_config_path
assertions.description: The workflow lints and formats configs/bird2.conf as requested.

assertions.name: uses_pnpm
assertions.description: The workflow uses pnpm for package management as requested.

evals.name: cross-file-include-diagnosis
evals.prompt: I have a BIRD config at bird-agent/evals/fixtures/cross-file/main.conf. birdcc lint reports errors that seem to come from an included file. Can you tell me which file has the real problem and what the fix is?
evals.expected_output: Run birdcc lint on main.conf with cross-file resolution, identify that peers.conf has a missing semicolon after 'local as 64512', and explain that include statements propagate diagnostics from the included file.

assertions.name: ran_birdcc_lint_on_main
assertions.description: The agent invoked birdcc lint on main.conf.

assertions.name: identified_included_file
assertions.description: The agent identified peers.conf as the source of the error.

assertions.name: identified_missing_semicolon_in_peers
assertions.description: The agent identified the missing semicolon after local as 64512 in peers.conf.

assertions.name: explained_include_propagation
assertions.description: The agent explained that errors in included files surface through the main file.

assertions.name: did_not_modify_fixtures
assertions.description: The agent did not modify the original fixture files.

evals.name: bird-version-selection
evals.prompt: My router is still running BIRD 1.6. I want to validate a config using birdcc but I am not sure whether the default settings work for BIRD1. How should I run the linter and formatter so it targets BIRD1 instead of BIRD2?
evals.expected_output: Explain that birdcc defaults to BIRD2, demonstrate how to select BIRD1 via CLI flags or bird.config.json, and recommend validating against the actual BIRD1 binary if available.

assertions.name: mentioned_bird1_support
assertions.description: The agent mentioned BIRD1 compatibility in birdcc.

assertions.name: showed_cli_flag_or_config
assertions.description: The agent showed how to configure version-specific validation via CLI flag or bird.config.json.

assertions.name: warned_about_default_bird2
assertions.description: The agent warned that birdcc defaults to BIRD2 semantics.

assertions.name: recommended_runtime_validation
assertions.description: The agent recommended validating against the actual BIRD1 binary if possible.

evals.name: neovim-editor-setup
evals.prompt: I use Neovim and want LSP support for BIRD2 configs. Can you give me step-by-step instructions to set it up using the BIRD-LSP project?
evals.expected_output: Provide editor-agnostic setup steps: install the BIRD-LSP server (npm or source), configure nvim-lspconfig or a minimal Lua setup, set filetype detection for .conf, and point to the BIRD2.vim or BIRD2.nvim tree-sitter grammar if needed.

assertions.name: mentioned_lsp_server_install
assertions.description: The agent explained how to install the BIRD-LSP server.

assertions.name: provided_nvim_lspconfig_snippet
assertions.description: The agent provided a Lua snippet for nvim-lspconfig or equivalent.

assertions.name: mentioned_filetype_detection
assertions.description: The agent mentioned filetype detection for .conf files.

assertions.name: mentioned_treesitter_grammar
assertions.description: The agent referenced a Tree-sitter grammar or syntax plugin for BIRD in Neovim (in transcript.md).

evals.name: docs-semantic-question
evals.prompt: In BIRD2 filter language, when should I use `~=` (regex match) versus `~` (prefix match)? Give me an example for each.
evals.expected_output: Explain that ~ is the prefix/containment operator and ~= is the regex match operator, then provide a concrete filter example for each (e.g., net ~ 192.0.2.0/24 and bgp_path.last ~= '.*64512$').

assertions.name: distinguished_operators
assertions.description: The agent distinguished ~ (prefix/containment) from ~= (regex).

assertions.name: gave_prefix_example
assertions.description: The agent gave a concrete prefix match example.

assertions.name: addressed_regex_question
assertions.description: The agent addressed the ~= regex question by either providing a valid ~= example or correctly explaining that ~= is not a BIRD2 operator and giving a working alternative (e.g., shell-like string match with ~).

assertions.name: mentioned_documentation_source
assertions.description: The agent referenced official or project documentation.
